title: Read-only results and checks separation
conclusion: Official documentation exposes simulation result Statistics and correlation generation as result-view operations, while submission checks are distinct platform tests. Alpha Mining OS should preserve a read-only result acquisition boundary and never treat a pre-check as submission authorization.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Stats and Generate Correlation are documented separately from Check Submission and Submit Alpha tests.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Expose read-only pullback adapters separately from the admission/dispatch adapter.
open_questions: Exact current read endpoints are intentionally not published in this asset.

title: Parent-child Multi protocol is an asynchronous contract
conclusion: Forum implementations treat a Multi-Simulation as a parent process with child statuses, queue completion and exception handling. This supports a generic parent/child state machine, but endpoints, status names and retry cadence remain unverified and must not be hard-coded.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Community implementations use queues, child results, cancellation and exception logging.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Implement parent and child state records, idempotent result materialization, timeout classes and explicit UNKNOWN terminal states.
conflicts: The cited implementations include direct calls that are not approved for Alpha Mining OS.
open_questions: Official parent/child lifecycle and cancellation semantics.

title: 429 and rate limiting must be centralized
conclusion: A high-vote forum post reports rate-limit and retry signals around correlation retrieval. The durable conclusion is not any numeric limit: honor server retry guidance, coordinate concurrent workers, enforce a shared budget, and fail closed when state or audit is missing.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Forum evidence recommends respecting Retry-After-style guidance and coordinating concurrent requests.
alpha_mining_os_impact: One scheduler owns token buckets, backoff and audit; adapters cannot self-retry around the gate.
conflicts: Reported numerical limits are intentionally omitted as unverified and mutable.
open_questions: Current server limits and whether they differ by entitlement or operation.

title: Batch construction needs a hard profile invariant
conclusion: Official Multi documentation requires Region, Delay, Language and Instrument Type to be common within a batch. A plan builder should partition candidates by those fields before batch assembly and reject mixed profiles prior to execution.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Official Multi setup requires the four fields to be the same across batch members.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Use a canonical SimulationProfile hash as the batching key and admission precondition.
open_questions: Does current platform additionally require same Universe or other settings?

title: Dataset and field metadata need typed compatibility
conclusion: Official data documentation defines Dataset as a collection of Fields and distinguishes matrix from vector fields, which must be converted before matrix use. This supports synchronizing dataset/field metadata with type and availability constraints rather than treating field names as free text.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Dataset is a collection of fields; vector fields require vector operators before use with matrix fields.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Snapshot datasets, fields, types and compatible operators; reject stale or incompatible generation plans.
open_questions: How entitlement and region availability are surfaced in the authoritative catalogue.

title: Operator and settings catalogues are admission inputs
conclusion: Official documents describe simulation settings and SuperAlpha selection properties, including field/operator-related resource constraints. These are catalogue facts that should be snapshot-versioned and validated at admission rather than embedded in prompts or copied forum code.
author: OFFICIAL_DOCUMENT
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Settings semantics and SuperAlpha operator constraints are documented, with warnings for resource truncation.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Maintain a platform registry snapshot with capabilities, settings, datasets, fields and operators.
open_questions: Current full settings catalogue and dynamic operator availability.

title: AI-generated expressions require pre-admission validation
conclusion: Forum experience shows batch status alone can hide per-child expression failures. The reusable design is an isolated validator that returns per-candidate states and errors before expensive research stages; direct-platform validation and credential-handling code must not be copied.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The post observes that a batch-level error can coexist with child-level outcomes and proposes an expression checker.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Adopt a local deterministic syntax/registry gate; any future remote validation requires explicit human authorization and the shared adapter.
conflicts: Source code contains credentials and real execution paths, prohibited by project governance.
open_questions: Which validation errors can be determined entirely offline?

title: Safety gate excludes community automation code
conclusion: Several forum sources contain automation patterns for simulation, correlation retrieval or submission. They are evidence of user practice only. Alpha Mining OS must not adopt their credentials, direct endpoints, auto-submit flows or polling bypasses; its API Gate and audit chain remain mandatory.
author: N/A
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Community code is not an authorization source; project constitution requires human approval, gate, adapter and audit.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Explicit non-adoption rule; permit only sanitized design patterns after review.
open_questions: None; this is a governance boundary.

title: Snapshot-before-admission is locally evidenced
conclusion: Local Alpha Mining OS platform-registry code is manifest-driven and preserves the previous active snapshot on retrieval/import failure. It validates fields and compatible operators against the active snapshot and reserves a snapshot/profile hash on a candidate plan.
author: LOCAL_REPOSITORY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Code comments and interfaces show active/staging/previous snapshots, compatibility validation and candidate-plan reservation.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Retain this design; add test coverage and keep it outside public source export.
open_questions: Runtime deployment status is not asserted by this source.

title: MCP, forum RAG and Research Memory adoption status
conclusion: UNKNOWN for a production integration. The curated forum corpus demonstrates value in source-indexed retrieval, but no official evidence was captured for a supported WorldQuant MCP contract. Use the local RAG only as a research source; do not make it a production fact source or execution dependency.
author: N/A
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Source-indexed retrieval is useful for review, while governance requires production decisions to be explicit and auditable.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Allow RAG/Research Memory as a read-only advisory input with provenance; never let it bypass admission, scheduler or execution adapter.
open_questions: Official MCP tool scope, authentication model and supported operations.

title: Client interruption is not evidence of slot release
conclusion: A forum implementation reports that terminating its local process can leave platform-side simulations running and occupying slots. This supports an UNKNOWN-after-interruption parent state and reconciliation before reuse. It does not authorize remote cancellation, deletion, or direct polling code.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Author reports local interruption did not necessarily stop a running simulation and then shows unsafe direct cancellation code.
alpha_mining_os_impact: On worker crash or restart, retain parent/child state as UNKNOWN and reconcile through an approved read adapter before slot allocation. Never infer capacity release from client exit.
conflicts: No official lifecycle or cancellation contract was found.
open_questions: What is the official post-interruption state and slot-release timing?
Does cancellation affect quota or error accounting?

title: AI template expansion needs field-first guardrails
conclusion: A high-vote forum practice describes using an AI assistant to infer a template's economic meaning and search for analogous fields or datasets. It also reports high correlation and operator-budget issues. The reusable design is a field-first, registry-validated proposal workflow with human review, never autonomous cross-dataset submission.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Author reports semantic template expansion, then correlation and operator-budget problems requiring manual adjustment.
alpha_mining_os_impact: AI may propose field-family substitutions only from the active registry; admission must revalidate field type, operator availability, settings and correlation evidence.
conflicts: Forum examples include direct execution claims and formula text that are excluded.
open_questions: Which semantic similarity signal is reliable enough to prioritize fields?
What is the current operator budget for each Alpha type?

title: Forum MCP systems support component boundaries only
conclusion: Several community prototypes divide work among tool transport, analysis/agent logic and local memory or task routing. Their direct platform automation and credential handling are non-adoptable; the only reusable design is to isolate advisory planning, deterministic validation, controlled scheduling and audited result materialization.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Posts describe layered tooling, agent decision support and task routing, while also including unsafe direct-execution material that is excluded.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Retain the existing chain: source snapshot -> candidate plan -> hard admission -> shared scheduler -> execution adapter -> result/audit. MCP/RAG may advise the plan only and cannot invoke execution or mutate gates.
conflicts: Forum prototypes claim unattended execution and use credentials or direct calls, which conflict with Alpha Mining OS authority boundaries.
open_questions: Which read-only tools can be approved behind the API Gate?
What evidence is sufficient to promote a workflow pattern from research memory to business code?

title: FastExpr-to-Python conversion needs semantic-parity evidence
conclusion: Forum conversion guides report that a literal translation can diverge because execution models, missing-value behavior, history windows and state differ. The reusable rule is to preserve an explicit semantic contract and validate parity in an authorized offline harness; no community correlation value, operator mapping, or code fragment is adopted as a general rule.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Authors identify operator semantics, data history, state and missing-value behavior as conversion risks and describe comparison-based validation.

applicability.Alpha_type: Python candidate derived from a FastExpr idea

alpha_mining_os_impact: Add a conversion-review artifact with field/type availability, history requirement, output contract, parity test outcome and unresolved semantics. Do not enqueue a converted candidate based on source text alone.
conflicts: Reported implementation shortcuts and numerical parity results vary by source and are not platform documentation.
open_questions: What approved offline parity metrics and acceptance bands should apply?
Which operator semantics have current official Python references?

title: Cache, incremental collection and batching are reusable only as offline patterns
conclusion: A community script combines caching, incremental retrieval, batching and pruning, but it also contains direct authentication and platform-access material. Cache keys, bounded batching, idempotent writes and explicit freshness are reusable engineering ideas; the script, endpoint logic and credential flow are non-adoptable.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The source combines cache, incremental state and batch handling with prohibited direct account access.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Use cache-keyed idempotency, source-versioning, explicit freshness and bounded worker concurrency only inside approved local collectors and adapters. Never copy the source's authentication or request logic.
conflicts: Source embeds direct authentication and access patterns forbidden by project policy.
open_questions: What immutable fields form each local cache key?
What expiry and refresh policy fits each read-only artifact kind?

title: Controlled mutation and provenance are safer than opaque optimization loops
conclusion: A forum workflow describes changing one dimension at a time, grouping small variant batches and retaining a trace of observations. The durable practice is controlled experiments with immutable inputs and audit records; its platform-specific checks, thresholds and submission steps are not adopted.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Posts advocate incremental variation, batch comparison and recording observations, but also state unverified outcome and threshold claims.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Store parent hypothesis, one controlled change, registry snapshot, observed result reference and next decision separately. Hard admission remains independent of the research loop.
conflicts: Forum workflow success claims and numeric limits are not independently verified.
open_questions: How many variants are affordable under the approved budget?
Which metrics can be retained in private audit without entering public review assets?

title: Research failure states need an infrastructure branch
conclusion: A forum research-loop post separates candidate quality states from timeout, queueing and backpressure conditions. This supports recording infrastructure states independently so a transient platform condition is not treated as evidence that an Alpha hypothesis failed. Its colors, thresholds and stop rules are not adopted.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The source distinguishes a research red light from an infrastructure problem and recommends a traceable experiment contract.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Use separate candidate, eligibility, throttled, pending, timeout and UNKNOWN states; only authorized reconciliation may change an infrastructure state.
conflicts: SE-003 covers shared rate-limit ownership; this source does not establish throttle scope or retry cadence.
open_questions: Which platform statuses map to infrastructure versus candidate failure?
What bounded reconciliation policy is permitted after an ambiguous result?

title: Capability discovery must be snapshot-bound rather than scraped
conclusion: Two forum scripts report that visible operators, fields and region/universe combinations can vary by account context. Their credential and direct-request implementations are non-adoptable. The reusable conclusion is to consume an authorized capability snapshot, version it and reject stale or incompatible plans locally.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The posts describe context-scoped operator and field inventories and include prohibited credential/direct-access code.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Extend the existing snapshot contract with scope, freshness and compatibility fields; never import community authentication or scraping code.
conflicts: SE-005 and SE-006 provide official type/catalogue support but do not guarantee a given account's current capability set.
open_questions: Which approved source supplies current capability snapshots?
What invalidates a cached field/operator capability record?

title: Correlation-family clustering is research triage, not admission
conclusion: A forum workflow groups a batch by pairwise PnL correlation and retains leading representatives per connected family. The useful pattern is candidate de-duplication and diversity review. Its suggested threshold, score formula and PnL handling are forum-specific and cannot reject or submit an Alpha.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The source recommends clustering similar batch outputs before comparing representatives inside each family.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Add an offline, versioned family-analysis stage after authorized results are available; preserve all candidates and mark selection as advisory.
conflicts: CM-003 and CM-004 define platform correlation terms separately from this local batch-analysis pattern.
open_questions: What local correlation representation is approved for candidate-family analysis?
How should missing or unequal observation windows be handled?

title: Multiple research inputs should converge before the execution boundary
conclusion: A forum dual-mode Python workflow accepts either an expression-like input or a source-file-like input and then routes both through one submission implementation. Its direct request and polling logic are non-adoptable. The reusable architecture is a single normalized candidate contract before admission, scheduling and any authorized adapter call.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The source describes two input forms sharing one downstream pipeline; it also contains prohibited direct platform access logic.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Normalize inputs into an immutable CandidatePlan and enforce the same hard admission, budget and audit chain for every source form.
conflicts: SE-014 requires semantic-parity evidence for language conversion before any plan is eligible.
open_questions: Which input forms may enter the research planner?
How are source-file provenance and static validation recorded without retaining private code?

title: Static dependency inventory is a CandidatePlan preflight, not an execution lane
conclusion: A forum parser extracts operator and data-field dependencies from an expression-like input. The reusable design is a non-executing dependency manifest resolved against an approved registry snapshot; parsing does not prove semantic validity, scope compatibility or execution authorization.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The post separates extraction of operators and fields from later use of the expression.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Add a private static dependency manifest to CandidatePlan review, then fail closed on unknown field type, operator, scope or parser ambiguity. Never expose source expressions in public knowledge assets.
conflicts: A parser can misclassify syntax or aliases and cannot replace registry or semantic-parity validation.
open_questions: Which grammar variants require a separate parser path?
How should extracted dependencies be versioned when a registry snapshot changes?

title: Field-family similarity research needs scope-bound cache and uncertainty labels
conclusion: A Brain Labs community workflow caches field observations and compares candidate fields within a selected Region, Delay and Universe. It supports scope-bound local research caching and similarity-as-hypothesis only; field similarity is neither Alpha correlation nor an admission result.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The author reports caching scoped field data and treating similarities as a search aid, with coverage and timing limitations.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Use immutable cache keys that include profile scope, field identity, source version and observed-at time. Store similarity as a proposal signal with coverage metadata, not as an admission or correlation verdict.
conflicts: Forum measurement choices and source coverage are not an official platform contract.
open_questions: Which approved local observation source can populate a field cache?
What freshness and coverage minimum are needed before a similarity result is shown to a researcher?

title: LLM and MCP assistants need a sanitized-plan boundary
conclusion: Forum workflows use MCP or LLM assistance for template discovery, optimization narratives and submission descriptions, but examples also traverse full expressions, credentials or direct platform actions. The reusable design is a strict boundary: an assistant receives only a sanitized CandidatePlan and approved metadata, and may return a draft research note or description without platform authority.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Sources describe AI-assisted template evaluation, optimization and description drafting while containing non-adoptable access or execution examples.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Expose AI only to redacted hypotheses, role placeholders and approved registry facts. It cannot receive credentials, full private expressions, account results or an execution capability, and cannot alter admission, budget or scheduler state.
conflicts: Source examples propose direct authentication or simulation actions that conflict with Alpha Mining OS authority boundaries.
open_questions: Which CandidatePlan fields are safe for an external model?
What review record is required before an AI-generated description is shown to a human?

title: Continuous Alpha research needs a control-plane boundary
conclusion: A community Pipeline MCP design distinguishes research skills from pipeline scheduling, governance, feedback and recovery. Its platform-facing actions are non-adoptable, but the architecture corroborates a control-plane boundary: advisory research creates candidate plans while deterministic gates and shared scheduling own all state transitions.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The post frames research, scheduling, governance, feedback and recovery as different parts of a production control plane.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Preserve the existing chain: bounded retrieval -> CandidatePlan -> hard admission -> shared scheduler -> execution adapter -> result and audit. Skills, RAG and MCP can propose work but cannot own execution state or platform authority.
conflicts: The source advocates platform-facing automation that conflicts with Alpha Mining OS real-WQ safety rules.
open_questions: Which control-plane states can be shared with a research assistant without leaking private data?
How should recovery decisions be reconciled after an authorized adapter reports an ambiguous state?

title: Research identity and lifecycle need a durable private ledger
conclusion: A forum MVP records canonical candidate identity and separate lifecycle states to avoid duplicated work across manual and tool-assisted research paths. Its platform-access implementation is excluded. The reusable conclusion is a private, idempotent ledger keyed by a sanitized candidate fingerprint and profile version; it does not authorize result retrieval or execution.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The author separates duplicate detection and lifecycle recording from the act of initiating a backtest.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Persist an immutable candidate fingerprint, profile hash, source reference, lifecycle state and audit pointer before scheduling. Keep source expressions and platform identifiers private; never let a ledger create a bypass to an adapter.
conflicts: The source includes platform-facing workflow details that are prohibited by the project boundary.
A local duplicate match cannot prove platform-side identity or result equivalence.
open_questions: Which canonicalization rules are safe across expression-like and source-file-like candidates?
What retention and deletion policy applies to private ledger references?

title: Python research needs an explicit semantic-state and data-quality contract
conclusion: Three forum posts identify state/history, missing-value and numeric-type handling, data adjustments and resource cost as distinct causes of Python research divergence. This is FORUM_EXPERIENCE, not a platform contract. A Python proposal therefore needs a private semantic-parity and resource review before it can enter the normal CandidatePlan path.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Posts describe history/state, missingness, numeric representation and compute cost as separate verification dimensions when representing a research idea in Python.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Require a private contract for field type, state/history, missingness, data adjustment assumptions, output shape and bounded resource estimate. A failed parity check blocks the candidate rather than triggering autonomous repair or a real call.
conflicts: Forum descriptions of runtime and data semantics may be incomplete or dated.
None of the cited posts establishes current official Python behavior for every scope.
open_questions: Which official documents define the current Python data and state contracts?
Which offline fixtures safely cover missingness, warm-up and adjustment cases?

title: Out-of-sample distribution review is a private robustness stage
conclusion: A forum retrospective compares grouped out-of-sample distributions across scoped research variants and warns that in-sample strength can be unstable. The reusable architecture is a private robustness review with explicit scope and provenance. Its analysis code, access logic and reported outcomes are excluded, and it does not define a platform acceptance threshold.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The post contrasts point estimates with grouped distributional review across research scopes and warns against treating one in-sample result as durable.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Add a private robustness-review artifact after authorized results exist, recording scope, window definition, coverage and distribution summary reference. It remains advisory and cannot override verified hard admission or expose account result data.
conflicts: The source contains prohibited access and result material.
A private distributional review does not establish official OS semantics or a universal acceptance band.
open_questions: Which private result representations are permitted for robustness review?
What evidence distinguishes a research robustness warning from an official platform check?

title: Research lineage needs private artifacts and a sanitized decision projection
conclusion: Several forum workflows retain an origin, variants, outcome classes, and next-step narrative. The reusable pattern is an append-only private research ledger plus a separate sanitized decision projection; source expressions, identifiers, raw results, and full run artifacts must not enter public review context.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Posts describe retaining lineage and compact summaries after long-running or interrupted research, while their raw examples contain private candidate material.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Store private CandidatePlan lineage, state, and audit references separately from sanitized review notes. A public artifact may name a hypothesis and terminal class but cannot contain a full expression, account result, or raw platform payload.
conflicts: Source examples retain direct candidate and result details that conflict with the public asset contract.
open_questions: Which private ledger fields are necessary for resume and dedupe?
Which aggregate decision fields are safe for external AI review?

title: Concurrency intent and worker execution need separate durable ownership
conclusion: Community implementations separate a control record from worker processes and report throttling after unbounded fan-out. This supports one scheduler-owned desired-concurrency state, worker heartbeats, and explicit reconciliation. It does not establish any platform capacity, queue scope, or retry cadence.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Posts describe control/worker separation, runtime adjustment, and a reported concurrent-request throttle, without an authoritative capacity contract.

applicability.Alpha_type: offline scheduler and future authorized adapter

alpha_mining_os_impact: Keep desired concurrency, leases, heartbeats, and recovery in the shared scheduler. A worker cannot set its own platform budget; a throttle response becomes a retryable infrastructure state, not a quota value.
conflicts: CM-018 keeps all forum quota and reset narratives UNKNOWN.
Source implementations include direct access patterns that are non-adoptable.
open_questions: What operations share a platform-side concurrency scope?
What approved observation proves an in-flight action was released or reconciled?

title: Automated candidate repair must create a reviewable new version
conclusion: Forum repair tools show that generated candidates can fail on operator signatures, argument forms, or field-shape assumptions. The safe design inference is to resolve each suggested repair against a versioned registry and create a new CandidatePlan version; ambiguous repairs fail closed and never mutate, simulate, or submit automatically.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: One post proposes rewrite rules and another reports data-shape ambiguity; neither is an official operator contract or a safe autonomous mutation path.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Require a syntax, signature, field-type, and semantic-delta record before a repaired candidate reaches admission. Preserve original and proposal fingerprints privately; block unknown operator or shape semantics.
conflicts: Community claims about documentation or runtime behavior may be stale or scope-specific.
open_questions: Which registry snapshot owns an operator signature?
What semantic comparison is sufficient for a proposed repair?

title: Template taxonomies should store role hypotheses before formula construction
conclusion: Forum template discussions organize two-input ideas by relationship, role, time structure, conditioning, and topology. These tags are useful for a research-plan taxonomy, but they do not prove low correlation, field compatibility, or a reusable formula. Public assets should retain only role placeholders.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Posts describe template families and structured labels rather than a portable, validated expression library.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Represent template intake with role tags and a human-reviewed economic hypothesis. Instantiate fields or operators only after an approved private registry snapshot and hard admission.
conflicts: A relationship taxonomy can be mistaken for an executable template or a low-correlation claim.
open_questions: Which role vocabulary is stable across datasets and Regions?
Which taxonomy tags improve diversity without becoming automated exclusion rules?

title: Execution-capable WQ command tooling is a prohibited integration pattern
conclusion: Local governance prohibits direct real-WQ calls, credentials, account writes, and bypasses around the approved adapter. Forum projects that expose authentication, simulation, result, correlation, metadata mutation, or submit operations therefore serve only as a security threat model, not an adoption source.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: Source projects describe direct platform-control capabilities; the project constitution forbids adopting those paths into Alpha Mining OS.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Do not vendor, invoke, translate, or expose the source implementations. Preserve only neutral requirements for an eventually human-authorized adapter: audit, bounded state, idempotency, explicit approval, and fail-closed recovery.
conflicts: The source projects promote autonomous platform operations, which conflict with the project authority boundary.
open_questions: What minimum audited interface is required before a future human-authorized read-only adapter can exist?
Which normalized state fields can be tested entirely with local mocks?

title: Research campaigns need stage-specific exit artifacts
conclusion: A practitioner separates low-cost probing, reusable-core definition, controlled scaling, focused polishing, and final delivery. This is a personal workflow, not a platform process. The reusable system pattern is a private research campaign with explicit stage transitions and artifacts; its final public-facing stage is an approval packet, not an execution command.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The author describes distinct exploration, core, scale, repair, and delivery phases with separate retained learning artifacts.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Model private campaign states as PROBE, CORE, SCALE, POLISH, and APPROVAL_PACKET. Each transition needs an immutable record and bounded local budget. The campaign cannot submit, modify an account, or bypass CandidatePlan, hard admission, scheduler, adapter, and audit.
conflicts: The source mixes personal simulations, result observations, and submission behavior that are outside the permitted system boundary.
open_questions: Which private artifact schema and retention policy support each campaign transition?
How should a CandidatePlan relate to a campaign core without allowing one campaign to create an execution path?

title: Correlation caches must stay advisory and scope-bound
conclusion: Forum code proposes fingerprinting previously observed high-correlation patterns and expiring a local cache. It does not prove that a fingerprint predicts Self, Power Pool, or Prod correlation. The reusable pattern is an advisory observation cache keyed by a private candidate fingerprint, correlation kind, profile scope, observed-at time, freshness, and opaque outcome state; it cannot reject a candidate or replace an authorized observation.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The author proposes an expiring local cache based on candidate signatures after observed correlation outcomes, without a paired validation study or official metric contract.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Permit only an advisory private ObservationCache behind the shared research ledger. Preserve kind, scope, freshness, provenance, and an opaque result class; do not store public expressions or result values. Cache hits may prioritize human review but cannot decide admission, budget, or any real request.
conflicts: CPE-012 and prior correlation evidence prohibit monotonic or one-metric screening assumptions.
The source conflates similarity-style signatures with future correlation outcomes without an official definition or calibration.
open_questions: Which private fingerprint can be retained without reconstructing an expression?
What held-out precision, recall, and freshness decay would justify advisory prioritization?
Can a cache key ever be compared across regions, delays, universes, or correlation kinds?

title: Research orchestration needs one durable state owner
conclusion: A practitioner describes a multi-role research workflow with a single session owner, phase checkpoints, recovery notes, and guards. The reusable conclusion is a private research controller that owns state and delegates bounded work; its direct platform automation claims are non-adoptable.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The post separates a state-owning orchestrator from data scouting, hypothesis work, validation, and execution roles.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Introduce a private CampaignSession with one state owner, explicit phase transitions, checkpoint references, bounded child reports, and an UNKNOWN recovery state. Research roles may propose work but cannot call an adapter, change a budget, or represent an external action as complete.
conflicts: The source mixes direct platform automation and personal workflow material that conflict with the project authority boundary.
No official platform lifecycle or research-phase contract is established.
open_questions: Which immutable fields define a CampaignSession checkpoint?
How should conflicting child reports be reconciled without losing audit lineage?
Which restart fixtures prove phase recovery is idempotent?

title: MCP tools need bounded contracts and cannot own workflow control
conclusion: Two practitioners report that broad tool responses pollute model context and that one-shot MCP calls are unsuitable as the durable controller of a multi-stage workflow. The reusable design is a narrow typed tool contract that returns bounded summaries or task references while durable state stays in the scheduler and research ledger.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The sources favor constrained inputs and concise outputs, and distinguish a tool call from durable workflow ownership.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Expose only read-only, redacted metadata and draft-note MCP contracts with typed inputs, bounded output, pagination or task references. The scheduler, ledger, admission gate, and adapter retain state and authority; tools never receive credentials, raw expressions, results, or execution capability.
conflicts: The sources also describe direct BRAIN access and automation, which are prohibited integration patterns.
No source establishes a current platform MCP schema or authorization model.
open_questions: Which response-size budget preserves useful context without hiding material state?
Which tool outputs need a durable task reference rather than inline content?
How should read-only capability snapshots be versioned for assistant use?

title: Disabling client-side throttling waits is a prohibited patch pattern
conclusion: A community post suggests editing a local dependency to suppress a wait associated with a rate-limit path. This is not reliability evidence or an acceptable repair. Under project governance, throttle and pending states remain explicit external conditions handled only by an approved, versioned adapter and test harness.
author: REDACTED_LOCAL_ONLY
excerpt_or_paraphrase: The post presents a manual change intended to suppress a wait in an MCP-side dependency.
alpha_mining_os_impact: Do not patch vendor or community client cooldowns, error guards, or polling behavior to change runtime behavior. Classify throttled, pending, and unknown state in the approved adapter; test them through local mocks and fail closed when audit or authority is absent.
conflicts: The source does not establish a current platform rate limit, retry scope, or error contract.
A local client modification cannot prove that an external action is safe or complete.
open_questions: Which local test doubles cover throttle, retry guidance, pending work, and crash recovery?
What version-review process is required before any approved adapter change?
